---
title: "Introduction to TVB modeling framework and The Virtual Brain Ontology for reproducible and comparable computational modeling"
subtitle: "Summer School 2026"
date: today
authors:
  - name: "Leon Martin"
affiliations:
  - id: 1
    name: "Charité University Medicine Berlin · Berlin Institute of Health"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    width: 1244
    height: 750
    dpi: 500
    fig-dpi: 500
    slide-number: true
    scrollable: true
    auto-stretch: true
    auto-play-media: true
    chalkboard: true
    incremental: false
    toc: false
    logo: img/Charite.png
    footer: "© Leon Martin · Charité University Medicine Berlin"
    link-external-newwindow: true
    code-line-numbers: true
    highlight-style: github
    include-in-header: _slides-home-btn.html
    css: slides.css
    title-slide-attributes:
      data-background-iframe: brain-network-background.html
      data-background-color: "#6880af"
      data-background-opacity: "1"
engine: jupyter
jupyter: python3
---

**Overview**:

- **Intro to Brain Network Modelling** — connectomes, local dynamics, coupling
- **Defining a FAIR Brain Network Model** — ontology-backed specs with TVB-O
- **Setup & First Examples** — a runnable environment
- **Bifurcation Analysis** — reading the dynamics as a map of regimes
- **Stimulating the Brain** — evoked responses & parameter inference

{{< include slides/_01-intro.qmd >}}

{{< include slides/_02-fair-model.qmd >}}

<!-- {{< include slides/_03-setup.qmd >}} -->

{{< include slides/_04-clinical.qmd >}}

{{< include slides/_05-bifurcation.qmd >}}

<!-- Parameter Exploration & Inference — not part of this course.
     Uncomment the next line to add it back. -->
<!-- {{< include slides/_06-parameter-exploration.qmd >}} -->

{{< include slides/_07-stimulation.qmd >}}


# Wrap-up {background-color="#6880af"}

## Putting it together {.unnumbered}

One reproducible workflow, from structure to mechanism:

**connectome → local dynamics → coupling → regimes → fitting → stimulation**

::: {.incremental}
- A brain network model = structure ($A_{ij},\ \tau_{ij}$) · local dynamics $f$ · coupling $g$ · observation $h$
- **TVB-O** captures all of it as FAIR, machine-readable metadata — reproducible *and* comparable
- **Bifurcation analysis** identifies the dynamical regime *before* you fit
- **Inference & stimulation** turn the model into testable, mechanistic hypotheses
:::

## Acknowledgements

::: {.r-stretch}
{{< include authors_gallery.qmd >}}
:::

## Thank You & Feedback {.r-fit-text}

::::{.rows}
:::{.row}
![Your Feedback](img/survey_qr.png){fig-align=center height=200px}
:::

:::{.row}
::: {style="text-align: center;"}
**Leon Martin**\
leon.martin [at] bih-charite.de
:::
:::

:::{.row}
| ![](img/Charite.png){fig-alt="Charite logo" width=30%} | ![](img/BIH.png){fig-alt="BIH logo" width=30%} | ![](img/BSS.png){fig-alt="BSS logo" width=30%} |
|:---:|:---:|:---:|
:::

::::

## References {.unnumbered}

::: {#refs}
:::

## {.unnumbered}
